factors.name: Fed Policy (Warsh FOMC decision imminent)
factors.summary: First decision under new Chair Kevin Warsh expected imminently; market consensus leans toward hold, but early-cycle uncertainty is elevated. Options traders are 'increasingly divided' on near-term rate path per Bloomberg, indicating low conviction ahead of announcement.

factors.name: US-Iran Peace Deal & Oil Prices
factors.summary: Oil has shed >7% across two sessions on deal expectations; prices at three-month lows. Lower energy costs are net-negative for inflation and real yields, but downward commodity shock can crimp industrial margins (CAT, BA exposure) and energy holdings (CVX).

factors.name: Dow Composition & Earnings Sentiment
factors.summary: JPM +3.72%, Visa +2.89%, HD +2.20%, 3M +2.17% leading yesterday's close; financials rallied on rate-hold expectations. However, broader NASDAQ fell -1.15% and S&P -0.57%, suggesting sector rotation away from mega-cap tech into value/dividend plays—typical churn into FOMC uncertainty.

factors.name: Japan Economic Strength & Supply Chain
factors.summary: Japan May exports +17.0% y/y (beat +16.2% est.), machinery orders +8.7% m/m (crush -0.9% est.). Strong semiconductor/auto demand supports Dow tech/industrial supply chains but highlights global growth backdrop and potential JPY strength risk if BoJ hikes further.

factors.name: US Housing & Manufacturing Softness
factors.summary: US May housing starts 1.177M vs 1.430M expected (miss); ISM Manufacturing PMI not yet published but forward guidance muted. Softer housing data pressures HD, GE exposures and suggests cyclical momentum waning.

factors.name: Import Prices & Inflation Risk
factors.summary: US May import prices +1.9% vs +1.0% expected—modestly hot. Combined with lower oil, net CPI risk is muted; PPI likely benign, favoring financials and rate-sensitive dividend names in Dow.

factors.name: Dollar Strength & Multinational Translation
factors.summary: USD Index near 99.50, volatile around oil correlation. DXY weakness supporting commodities but Dow's 50% international revenue exposure benefits from stable/stronger dollar; no directional conviction yet.

factors.name: Tech Sector & Leverage Dynamics
factors.summary: NASDAQ approaching 100-hour MA support (26,380); S&P underperforming. SpaceX hype/mania (leveraged ETFs, options) is concentrated in non-Dow mega-caps; MSFT/GOOGL exposure in Dow 30 is limited. Broad tech weakness headwind but Dow financials/industrials providing some offset.

factors.name: Private Credit Stress & Default Risk
factors.summary: Private-credit defaults hit 3-year high per KBRA index; $1.8T market showing stress. Tail risk for credit cycles and financial institutions; JPM/GS profitability under pressure if credit deteriorates further.

factors.name: Geopolitical (Iran Peace Deal, G7 Tariff Talk)
factors.summary: US-Iran deal reduces war premium (oil down, gold down from $4,335 from near-record highs); positive for growth and inflation narrative. However, G7 agenda shows rising tariff threat on China exports—protectionism risk for Dow industrials with China supply chains (CAT, BA, BA).

factors.name: Market Positioning & Volatility
factors.summary: VIX and spreads stable; no acute panic but elevated option fragmentation ahead of FOMC. Dow's outperformance yesterday (+0.64% vs SPX -0.57%, NDX -1.15%) reflects rotation into dividend/value, but gains modest.

screening.narrative: Dow climbed 0.64% on June 16, led by JPM and cyclicals (Visa, HD, 3M), as market priced a Fed hold under Warsh ahead of the FOMC decision hours away. Broader indices (SPX, NDX) retreated, signaling rotation into Dow value/dividend names amid tech underperformance and leverage unwinding in high-growth names (SpaceX hype notwithstanding). Oil's 7%+ selloff on US-Iran peace deal reduces energy tail risk but also caps inflation concerns, supporting equities structurally but pressuring energy stocks and industrial margin expectations. Housing data disappointed, and private-credit defaults ticked to cycle highs—macro headwinds are building beneath the surface. Entry conviction is low because FOMC is imminent (hours away), and post-announcement volatility will likely dominate.
catalysts_ahead: FOMC decision & Warsh press conference (expected 2:00 PM ET June 18, 2026)
Post-FOMC market repricing (risk-on if dovish hold, risk-off if hawkish surprise)
ISM Services PMI (likely early-to-mid week)
Factory orders & durable goods orders (if not yet published)
Dow heavyweight earnings updates (UNH, GS, BA, CAT guidance)
Tariff/G7 developments (protectionism vs China)
screening.risk_warning: FOMC surprise toward tightening (unexpected hawkish signals) would flip bias sharply BEARISH and likely cap Dow upside as rate expectations reprice higher. Conversely, dovish surprise or explicit rate-cut signal could trigger rallies in dividend/cyclical names. Housing/labor data deterioration, credit event spillover, or oil rebound on Iran deal reversal would also shift calculus. Current phase is high-chop zone into event risk—late-cycle entry not recommended.
screening.entry_guidance: Avoid chasing; FOMC decision imminent and market is already in price-discovery mode. Wait for post-FOMC clarity and look for retest/pullback to 51,500–51,750 support zone before considering long entry on a confirmed Warsh dovish lean. If FOMC surprises hawkish, watch for breakdown below 51,000.

factors.name: Fed Policy & Rate Decision (TODAY)
factors.summary: FOMC decision under new Chair Kevin Warsh expected today with no rate change anticipated. Market positioning cautious ahead of announcement; significant catalyst risk within hours.

factors.name: US-Iran Peace Deal & Geopolitical Risk
factors.summary: Framework agreement reduces tail risk from Hormuz strait disruption, supporting crude oil normalization (WTI ~$75.80, down ~5% prior session). Lower energy input costs support industrials, but deal still requires final text confirmation.

factors.name: USD Weakness
factors.summary: DXY weakened to 99.50 on Iran peace deal optimism; multinational Dow names (50% intl revenue) suffer translation headwinds. USD weakness typically pressures exporters in near term.

factors.name: UK CPI & Global Inflation
factors.summary: UK May CPI held at 2.8% y/y (vs 3.0% expected), monthly figures slightly higher than forecast. No major US inflation surprise in news window; awaiting US retail sales data today.

factors.name: Japan Export Strength & Supply Chain
factors.summary: Japan May exports +17.0% y/y (beat +16.2% est), fastest pace since Nov 2022 on semiconductor/AI demand. Supports thesis for tech/capital equipment (Dow heavyweights CAT, BA, GE benefit from supply chain strength).

factors.name: China Credit & Growth Signals
factors.summary: PBOC Governor Pan Gongsheng signals structural downshift in credit growth—'both difficult and necessary to maintain prior pace.' CNY 300bn recapitalization bond signals banking stress; slower Chinese growth headwind for Dow cyclicals (CAT, DE, MMM).

factors.name: Oil Price & Energy Costs
factors.summary: WTI near $75.80, ~5% lower on peace deal hopes but still above pre-war baseline. Energy input-cost relief supportive but not dramatic; Dow has CVX exposure but lighter than Russell 2000.

factors.name: Tech & AI Momentum
factors.summary: SpaceX IPO debut strong ($800M Korean retail demand); JPMorgan Asset Mgmt sees 'more upside to tech trade' in AI data center supply chain. Supports MSFT, mega-cap tech exposure within Dow.

factors.name: Market Concentration & Volatility Risk
factors.summary: Bloomberg reports 'few giants destabilizing indexes' and notes 'safety in numbers isn't what it used to be.' Dow's price-weighted structure means any mega-cap correction (MSFT, GS, JPM, UNH) has outsized impact; tech concentration risk present.

factors.name: 10Y Treasury & Rate Expectations
factors.summary: Fed decision today is anchor; Warsh leadership adds uncertainty. Gold holding above $4,300 suggests stable real-yield expectations; no major bond rout evident in news.

factors.name: Short-Term Price Action
factors.summary: 4h change -0.27%, 24h change +0.03%; minimal directional conviction. Consistent with NEUTRAL5/10 bias from 2026-06-17 00:15.

screening.narrative: Dow sits in narrow consolidation ahead of today's FOMC decision, with conflicting cross-currents: US-Iran peace deal and Japan export strength support cyclicals and tech, while China's signaled credit downshift and USD weakness pressure multinational translation. Geopolitical tail risk easing (oil lower) but concentrated mega-cap exposure (MSFT, JPM, GS, UNH) creates asymmetric downside if Fed messaging disappoints or tech rotates. Market is 'on guard' (per headlines) awaiting Warsh's first policy call.
catalysts_ahead: FOMC Decision & Warsh Press Conference (TODAY, likely 18:00 UTC/14:00 ET)
US May Retail Sales (expected today, TBD exact time)
US-Iran Deal Final Text Release (intraday, TBD)
ISM Manufacturing PMI (likely next week)
Dow Component Earnings Flow (UNH, MSFT, GS updates possible in coming days)
screening.risk_warning: FOMC hawkish surprise (rates higher, forward guidance tightens) would flip bias BEARISH and trigger sharp selloff, especially in rate-sensitive dividend payers (UNH, JNJ within Dow). Conversely, dovish Warsh surprise would lift risk appetite and confirm bullish break above 52,400. Iran deal text disappointment could reverse geopolitical relief. China growth slowdown spillover (lower commodity demand, EM stress) would pressure cyclicals (CAT, BA, GE).
screening.entry_guidance: Wait for FOMC outcome; bias too near-term uncertain to chase. If Fed holds/dovish, look for breakout above 52,200 with confirmation retest. If hawkish, treat any 52,100+ bounce as shorting opportunity.

factors.name: US-Iran Peace Deal
factors.summary: US-Iran framework agreement reached; planned MoU signing Friday in Switzerland. Oil prices collapsed 15% over 4 sessions (Brent below $79/bbl, WTI near $75.50), easing near-term inflation risk and transportation costs for Dow industrials and logistics names. Removes geopolitical risk premium from equity valuations.

factors.name: Fed Policy (Warsh First Meeting)
factors.summary: Fed expected to hold at 3.50-3.75% and remove easing bias per consensus. Warsh's first post-FOMC press conference today is critical signal; oil collapse reduces near-term inflation urgency but Q2 inflation data (Euro area +3.2% vs 3.0% prior, highest since Sept 2023) shows stickiness. No cuts priced for 2026; hawkish dot plot expected.

factors.name: Real Yields & Long-End Bonds
factors.summary: Oil-driven inflation expectations reversing lower, supporting bond prices and equity multiples. However, sticky services inflation in Eurozone and strong US growth narrative keep nominal yields anchored; higher government spending concerns remain per Bloomberg. Rate-sensitive Dow dividend payers (JNJ, PG, KO) benefit from stable yields, but cyclicals (CAT, BA) face headwind if growth fears mount.

factors.name: China Slowdown Risk
factors.summary: BMW issued profit warning citing China demand collapse and Middle East disruption; German automaker margin sliding to as low as 1%. Caterpillar, Boeing, and materials-heavy Dow names face structural drag from Chinese manufacturing weakness. No fresh PMI data in news window, but weakness is confirmed by major OEM guidance.

factors.name: Dollar Strength & FX Translation
factors.summary: USD consolidating after giving back >50% of post-May payrolls gains per BBH. Cyclical bullishness supports Dollar in medium term, but oil collapse and Fed hold remove short-term upside pressure. Dow's ~50% international revenue exposure means USD stability is net-neutral rather than tailwind today.

factors.name: Oil & Energy Input Costs
factors.summary: Brent crude -15% over 4 sessions; WTI near multi-week lows. Direct benefit to CVX and broader transportation/logistics cost structure. Aviation sector seeing debt boom confidence return (per Bloomberg); Airlines resume Middle East flights. Dow's diversified exposure benefits from lower energy-driven inflation.

factors.name: Equity Market Technicals & Sentiment
factors.summary: S&P 500 futures +0.26%, Nasdaq 100 futures +0.8% premarket (per 08:17 update), indicating broad risk-on tone. However, Nasdaq noted to stay 'under pressure as traders hedge into FOMC decision risk.' Dow 4h change -0.27%, 24h +0.03% — minimal momentum, no directional conviction. BofA noted investors sold equities last week but bought back into tech; flows mixed.

factors.name: Dow Earnings & Forward Guidance
factors.summary: No major Dow-30 earnings releases in news window. Bernstein raised Delta Air Lines (DAL) price target on demand strength, a positive micro signal for cyclical recovery. Apple investors losing patience with AI promises (per Bloomberg), signaling potential guidance disappointment ahead. Earnings season not in full swing; guidance vacuum limits conviction.

factors.name: Manufacturing & ISM Data
factors.summary: No fresh ISM Manufacturing or Services PMI in news window. Euro area industrial data shows weakness (BMW warning, German auto sector stress). US durable goods, factory orders, and industrial production data not reported today. Void of hard macro data limits ability to confirm cyclical health or deterioration.

factors.name: Geopolitical Risk Premium Reversal
factors.summary: Iran war risk premium unwinding rapidly post-framework deal. Strait of Hormuz reopening removes supply shock threat; tanker tracking shows Iranian oil exiting blockade zone. Equity risk-off bid should reverse; volatility (VIX) likely to compress further. Reduces tail-risk hedging demand.

factors.name: US Trade & Tariff Risk (G7 Context)
factors.summary: Trump at G7 threatens Iran ('go right back to dropping bombs') but peace deal already in hand. G7 agreed rare earths supply-chain diversification away from China (no single country >60% by 2030), signaling long-term strategic rebalancing. No immediate tariff escalation news; UK-India trade deal hints (per Starmer hot mic) suggest dealmaking momentum, not escalation.

factors.name: Credit Markets & Debt Issuance
factors.summary: Credit-default swaps show no stress despite $300B+ issuance YTD; investors 'keen to absorb supply' per MarketWatch. Financial conditions stable; aviation and infrastructure debt issuance accelerating. Dow financial names (JPM, GS, V) benefit from healthy credit conditions and deal flow.

screening.narrative: The Dow received a major geopolitical relief catalyst: US-Iran framework deal signed, oil collapsed 15%, easing inflation and risk-off sentiment. However, the rally is being tempered by three headwinds: (1) China slowdown confirmed by BMW profit warning and weak auto demand, directly hitting Dow cyclicals (CAT, BA); (2) Sticky Eurozone services inflation (+3.2% vs 3.0% prior, highest since Sept 2023) keeping Fed hawkish bias intact despite oil collapse; (3) FOMC decision imminent (today), creating pre-event hedge chop. The 24h move of +0.03% and 4h move of -0.27% show no conviction yet — market is waiting for Warsh's first post-FOMC press conference to reset rate expectations. Peace premium is bullish, but execution risk (China weakness, Fed hawkishness, earnings void) keeps confidence modest.
catalysts_ahead: FOMC Rate Decision & Warsh Press Conference (TODAY ~2pm ET) — expected hold at 3.50-3.75%, SEP dot plot and inflation projections
ISM Manufacturing PMI (likely this week) — will test whether China slowdown/auto weakness spreading to US industrial base
Durable goods orders, factory orders, industrial production data (likely this week) — confirm or deny cyclical durability post-oil relief
Dow-30 earnings releases (scattered through June) — Apple, Microsoft, Goldman Sachs guidance key for forward conviction
China PMI data (if released) — critical for CAT, BA, materials demand outlook
10Y Treasury yield response to FOMC hold & Warsh tone — will drive valuation and dividend-payer repricing
screening.risk_warning: FOMC can deliver a hawkish surprise (dot plot showing no cuts + higher inflation forecasts) that re-steepens yield curve and crushes dividend/rate-sensitive Dow names. Alternatively, China growth data could worsen sharply, triggering cyclical selloff in CAT, BA, MMM, HON. Oil could stabilize above $80 if Iran deal falters or supply cuts surprise; reflationary move would benefit energy but penalize broad equities. Move could extend lower if pre-event risk-off selling accelerates into Warsh presser.
screening.entry_guidance: Move is early-stage post-geopolitical relief but not yet extended; wait for FOMC decision at 2pm ET before committing. If Warsh is dovish (rate-cut hints), buy dips to 51900 support. If hawkish, sell rallies into 52200–52400 resistance. Current neutral tone suggests retest of support or wait-and-see mode ahead of event.

factors.name: Fed Policy & Warsh Debut
factors.summary: FOMC expected to hold rates at 3.50%-3.75% in unanimous decision, removing easing bias. Kevin Warsh's first meeting is a major wildcard — Fidelity warns of potential bond/volatility spikes; Senator Warren expresses discomfort. No shock move expected, but forward guidance (removal of dot plot?) could shift rate-cut expectations.

factors.name: Retail Sales (May)
factors.summary: US May retail sales +0.9% MoM vs +0.5% expected; ex-autos +0.8% vs +0.5% expected; y/y +6.9%. Control group +0.7% vs +0.4%. Strong consumer data supports demand for Dow dividend payers and cyclicals.

factors.name: Pending Home Sales
factors.summary: May pending home sales +3.8% MoM vs +0.8% expected, +4.6% YoY. Broad-based gains across all four US regions. Positive for housing-related Dow names (HD, DIS leisure) and financials (JPM, GS mortgage origination).

factors.name: Oil Prices & Iran Deal Framework
factors.summary: WTI crude has retreated to ~$76.50, down from spike on Iran framework deal (Strait of Hormuz reopening scheduled Friday). Lower energy prices reduce input costs for transportation/industrials but hurt CVX and energy exposure. Volatility remains high; Trump threatened 'bombing Iran' if he dislikes final deal terms.

factors.name: US Dollar & FX Dynamics
factors.summary: USD mixed on Fed Day; EUR retreated below 1.1600 on caution ahead of Warsh. USD/CAD up ~0.10%. Dollar strength would pressure multinational earnings translation (Dow ~50% foreign revenue), but FX remains choppy into FOMC.

factors.name: Tech Sector Divergence (Broadcom vs Microsoft)
factors.summary: BROADCOM surging (AI chip strength); MSFT tumbling (unspecified headwinds). SpaceX stock down 5% after multiday IPO rally (recently joined indices). Mixed signals within mega-cap tech — limits clear Dow narrative.

factors.name: Geopolitical & Trade Risks
factors.summary: Trump-Iran rhetoric ('go back to dropping bombs'), DNI nomination jeopardy (Jay Clayton hearing canceled), FISA/voting bill linkage threats. Elevated policy uncertainty and potential supply-chain disruption weigh on cyclicals (CAT, BA, MMM). BMW issued profit warning citing Iran war and China slowdown.

factors.name: China Growth & Demand Weakness
factors.summary: BMW slumped to 5-year low on China slowdown concerns combined with Iran war disruption. No fresh China PMI in this window, but sentiment deteriorating. Negative for Caterpillar, Boeing, and materials.

factors.name: Dow Component Earnings & Guidance
factors.summary: Limited fresh earnings in this window. J&J steering clear of obesity drugs (focus cancer) — strategic shift but not a shock. CarMax profit declined 4Q in a row; CME CEO transition announced (moderately bullish for orderly handoff). No major guidance bombs or surprises from heavyweight industrials yet.

factors.name: Credit & Volatility
factors.summary: Allianz CEO warns risk 'not properly priced.' Private credit valuations under scrutiny in Australia. Credit spreads not explicitly flagged as widening, but sentiment cautious. VIX not mentioned; Fed uncertainty likely keeping vol bid in.

factors.name: Inflation & Input Costs
factors.summary: No major CPI/PPI print in this window. ECB's Sleijpen says 2022-style inflation repeat 'less likely but can't be excluded.' Gasoline prices elevated (retail sales benefiting from 26.5% YoY pump price hike, not volume). Net input pressure remains moderate but watching.

factors.name: Bias History Continuity
factors.summary: Three consecutive NEUTRAL 5/10 reads (06/17 00:15, 06:09, 11:46) with no confirmation. Today's data (retail sales beat, pending homes beat, oil retreat, Iran/geopolitical noise) creates conflicting forces — still no clear edge.

key_levels.resistance: ~43,800–44,000 (recent highs before SPX worst session in 8 months)

screening.narrative: The Dow is caught between competing forces on Fed Day: stronger-than-expected May retail sales and pending home sales bolster the consumer and cyclical narrative, but geopolitical tensions (Trump's Iran rhetoric, DNI jeopardy, FISA threats), China slowdown concerns (BMW warning), and oil retreat inject uncertainty. Kevin Warsh's debut as Fed Chair is the primary wildcard — he may signal a hawkish tone or talk down rate-cut bets, which would pressure valuations and favor dollar strength. The market is pricing a unanimous hold and removal of easing bias, but Warsh's forward guidance could trigger repricing if he sounds less dovish than expected or hints at data-dependency shifting toward inflation concerns.
catalysts_ahead: FOMC decision & Warsh statement (TODAY, likely 14:00 ET)
Warsh post-meeting press conference and dot plot (if released; market focus on inflation/growth projections)
Iran deal final signing Friday (Strait of Hormuz formal deal, affects oil/supply chains)
ISM Manufacturing PMI and ISM Services PMI (next week — key Dow industrial health check)
Durable goods orders, factory orders (next week — cyclical confirmation)
Dow heavyweight earnings (UNH, MSFT, GS, HD, CAT, V, JPM, BA — ongoing season)
screening.risk_warning: FOMC could deliver a dovish surprise (reinstate easing bias, lower growth/inflation projections) → would flip bias BULLISH. Conversely, hawkish dot plot or Warsh rhetoric about sticky inflation/unemployment could drive equities lower and lift dollar/yields. Oil spike on Iran deal collapse or Trump military rhetoric could quickly shift energy and inflation calculus. China data next week could confirm or refute slowdown concerns; a weak PMI would pressure cyclicals hard. Retail sales beat may not sustain if gasoline-price tailwind reverses (26.5% YoY pump inflation masking weak real consumption).
screening.entry_guidance: Avoid chasing until FOMC conclusion and Warsh tone clarify (expect elevated chop 14:00–15:00 ET and 15:30 ET into press conference). If Warsh sounds hawkish, expect dip to support ~43,200–43,400 as repricing occurs; consider entry on pullback after initial sell-off settles. If dovish, resistance at ~43,800–44,000. Default stance: hold cash, wait for post-FOMC consolidation before committing.

factors.name: Fed Policy (Warsh Debut)
factors.summary: FOMC held rates steady at 3.50–3.75% as expected (unanimous vote). However, Warsh removed the 'easing bias' language from April statement and eliminated the dot plot projection, signaling a hawkish tilt. June dot plot shows 3.8% end-2026 vs. 3.4% in March—no cuts expected this year, vs. one cut previously projected.

factors.name: Rate Expectations / Real Yields
factors.summary: Hawkish repricing: market shifted from pricing 1-2 cuts in 2026 to zero cuts or potential hikes. Higher real yields pressure equities and Dow dividend payers. Long-end rates likely to drift higher if this narrative holds.

factors.name: Economic Activity & ISM Pending
factors.summary: Fed statement says economic activity expanding at 'solid pace' with strong productivity and capital investment. Job gains keeping pace; unemployment little changed. No major data misses visible in recent window. Manufacturing and Services PMI due Thursday—critical for confirmation.

factors.name: Inflation & Input Costs
factors.summary: Fed still cites 'elevated' inflation relative to target. Oil at ~$75.70 (stable, not deflationary). No CPI surprise in the news window, but geopolitical premium (Iran deal uncertainty, Middle East volatility) could add upside risk to energy/commodities, pressuring corporate margins for Dow industrials.

factors.name: Dollar Strength
factors.summary: Hawkish Fed typically supports DXY. ~50% of Dow revenue is international, so stronger dollar is a headwind for reported earnings translation. No major FX catalyst evident in news window, but rate-repricing should support greenback over next few days.

factors.name: Geopolitical & Trade (Iran Deal)
factors.summary: Trump-brokered Iran deal expected to be signed Friday, opening Strait of Hormuz and reducing Middle East uncertainty. Oil spike risks diminish. Positive for risk appetite and Dow aerospace/defense names (BA, RTX exposure). However, execution risk remains—Trump himself said deal 'might not' be one he should sign.

factors.name: Dow Component Earnings / Guidance
factors.summary: No major Dow 30 earnings or guidance releases in the news window. CarMax beat expectations but stock fell due to margin pressures and turnaround doubts. Mixed signals on consumer-facing cyclicals. Awaiting earnings from heavyweight names (UNH, JPM, GS, etc.).

factors.name: Credit / Risk Appetite
factors.summary: MarketWatch reports Wall Street reviving risky leveraged loan deals. Some appetite for risk remains, but no credit-market stress evident. VIX not mentioned as spiking; geopolitical risk premium likely modest after Iran deal headline.

factors.name: Retail Flows & Sentiment
factors.summary: Citadel Securities reports 'astronomical' retail flows on SpaceX IPO day and broader equity rally. Retail net-bought $369.8M SpaceX in first 3 days. Retail enthusiasm is tilting bullish, though concentration risk (SpaceX, AI names) is high.

factors.name: Price Action (4h / 24h)
factors.summary: US30 down 0.51% in 4h, down 0.62% in 24h. Modest weakness into and just after the FOMC announcement. No panic selling, but directional bias is downward. Move is small; no extended decline yet, but momentum is negative.

screening.narrative: The Dow opened mixed and drifted lower following Warsh's hawkish FOMC debut. While the rate decision was fully priced (hold at 3.50–3.75%), the removal of easing bias language and the dot plot revision (zero cuts in 2026 vs. one cut in March) represent a meaningful shift in Fed communication. This repricing of long-term rates is modestly bearish for the Dow's dividend- and rate-sensitive components. Offsetting factors include a solid economic backdrop (Fed's own language), the Iran deal resolution (reducing geopolitical risk), and strong retail inflows. However, with hawkish Fed expectations now embedded, the near-term bias is cautiously bearish until economic data (ISM due Thursday) or Dow heavyweight earnings provide fresh conviction.
catalysts_ahead: ISM Manufacturing PMI & Services PMI (Thursday) — critical to validate Fed's 'solid pace' narrative; weakness here would flip bias bearish
Dow component earnings calendar (UNH, JPM, GS, MSFT, V, CAT, GE, HON, etc.) — forward guidance from cyclical leaders (CAT, DE) critical for industrial demand outlook
10-year Treasury yield trending; market repricing of 2027–2028 rate path
Iran deal signing Friday — geopolitical risk resolution or execution disappointment
China PMI / economic data (week of 6/19) — major driver of Dow CAT, BA, materials exposure
CPI / PCE inflation data (scheduled for week of 6/24) — if hot, cements rate-hold narrative; if cool, could allow market to price in eventual cuts
screening.risk_warning: If economic data (ISM, retail sales, jobless claims) prints weaker than expected AND Powell / Warsh signal dovish hesitation on future hikes, the Dow could reverse sharply higher (real yields collapse, equities rally). Conversely, if inflation surprises hot and Fed signals 2–3 hikes in 2027, downside risk accelerates. Iran deal failure or renewed Middle East escalation could spike oil and volatility, pressuring the market. Dow's price-weighted structure means a single component earnings miss (e.g., CAT, BA, UNH) can move the index 100+ points.
screening.entry_guidance: Bias is neutral-to-bearish but very weak. Do not short aggressively; move is already -0.62% in 24h (Phase 1, not extended). Wait for 52,000–52,100 retest or fresh ISM data Thursday before committing. If ISM Manufacturing PMI <50 (contraction), lower bias to SELL with confidence 6–7. If ISM beats, reassess to BUY.
